dialogue: I have been studying how the missile classifies obstacles. I may have found a pattern exploit.
That sounded confident until the word may.
Diagonal rows. Three walls per row. It should confuse the pathfinder.
Dijkstra expands up, down, left, and right. Diagonals look clever to us, but they may not change the actual shortest route.
*outside, the team places Leonard's diagonal rows under a red sky*
If this fails, we lose setup time.
Missile entering the grid. It is ignoring the diagonal pattern.
Because the shortest path barely changed.
No more tricks. I will place walls that actually raise the route cost.
